stonetop.summary: Stonetop sits on a bluff overlooking the Great Wood, which stretches hundreds of miles to the east.

text.content: @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.6yScslDfqrcCQ6CJ]{Stonetop} sits on a bluff overlooking the Great Wood, which stretches hundreds of miles to the east. Few villagers dare to even cross @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.KJUzuYlurcjPxHd7]{the Stream} that flows near the base of the bluff. Hunters, trappers, and foragers go deeper, but even they venture a day or two into the forest at most.

Always

  • Massive trunks, reaching up up up, some bigger around than houses
  • Pale sky glimpsed through the canopy
  • Trees bending, creaking, rustling in the wind
  • A skittering in the distance
  • A sense of smallness, of being swallowed up by something ancient and vast

Spring

  • Feet slipping on mud, rotting leaves
  • Drip drip dripping of snowmelt, rain, mist
  • Pale buds and early blooms, blue and yellow and violet

Hopeful swell of birdsong

The terrain is hilly and rugged, laced with small streams and dotted with ponds. The trees are a mix of deciduous and evergreen. In some spots, the trees grow tightly, surrounded by thick brush so that one can barely walk. In others, massive old growth trees dominate, the space between them sparse and open.

Summer

  • Brilliant, overwhelming green
  • Drone of insects buzzing, birds chirping
  • Oppressive heat and humidity

Autumn

  • A world aflame with reds and golds
  • Crunch of needles and leaves underfoot
  • Stark long shadows cast by a low sun
  • Sharp smell of cold, damp air

Winter

  • A frosty coating, a deathly stillness
  • Shocking white of snow drifts
  • Trees like boney fingers
  • Surprising chirp and rustle of finches

For generations, the people of @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.6yScslDfqrcCQ6CJ]{Stonetop} have kept their promise to the Forest Folk: they never cut down a living tree in the Great Wood. The villagers gather deadfall, but timber comes from elsewhere.

  • According to the elders, when did someone last break the compact? What happened to them?
  • Who’s been pushing to start harvesting trees, since the Forest Folk seem to have vanished?

What will happen if someone fells a living tree? That’s for you to decide. Maybe…

  • … they suffer the Forest’s Wrath; … they risk releasing an evil spirit, held captive by the tree’s roots; … the trees house the spirits of dead
  • Forest Folk, and they’ve just created a specter; or … nothing—unless the Forest Folk return.

• Who in your family used to ply the
  • Wood? How did they lose their nerve?
  • What did you see deep in the Wood that you’ve never told to another?
  • Who did you lose to the Wood? How?
  • Of all the stories you’ve heard of the
  • Wood, which one scares you most?

• A villager wanders off into the Wood for unknown reasons
  • A hunter stumbles home, having narrowly escaped some new danger
  • A southern scholar arrives in @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.6yScslDfqrcCQ6CJ]{Stonetop}, seeking a guide into the Great Wood
  • A hunter or trapper who the PCs care about hasn’t returned home

Trappers are catching strange game: sickly, mutated, and wrong

. steading improvement .

GREAT WOOD TIMBER Requires all of the following:

  • Proof that trees can be felled without dire consequence
  • A crew of brave lumberjacks
  • A skilled carpenter, working full time
  • Pulling Together once, over a course of a few weeks, to generate the first load of timber.

When you mark all the requirements, increase Fortunes by 1 and add “Timber from the Great Wood” to the Resources list.

Henceforth, the steading has a ready supply of timber for improvements and other projects, and it consumes 1 less Surplus every winter.

Pick 1 or combine 2, or have someone roll the Die of Fate (d12).

RollResult
1Pond, wetland, or lake
2Creek, stream, or river
3Rocky outcropping, cave(s)
4–5Clearing, meadow, sparse trees
6–7Dense thicket
8–9Swath of one type of tree
10Briars, thorns, nettles
11Slope, hill, ridge, ravine
12Single, notable tree

If you like, roll 1d6 for size/scope/intensity.

For any of these, pick 1 or have someone roll the Die of Fate.

1d6 discovery

RollResult
1A change of terrain and roll again
2A chance for insight into a threat or danger (e.g. tracks that reveal numbers, whereabouts, etc.)
3game (see below)
4Useful or valuable flora
5A crinwin, Fae or spirit of the wild, curious or indifferent
6A point of interest

1d6 game

RollResult
1–3Mostly harmless but prone to bolt: deer, fowl, rabbits, etc.
4–5Big and dangerous if provoked: boars, wisents, tal gerdwyr, etc.
6Deadly and easily provoked: cave bears, ceirwmawr, raselbaedd, etc.

1d6 point of interest

RollResult
1A place corrupted by the Things Below or the Fomoraij
2A place haunted by death and the undying
3A place bearing traces of the Forest Folk
4A place sacred to the spirits of the wild
5A Fae domain or waystone
6A ruin or artifact of the Green Lords

If it’s been there for at least 10 years, it’s likely marked by Forest Folk glyphs.

Hazards

  • Getting lost
  • Treacherous terrain (loose footing, mudslides, sinkholes, deadfall, etc.)
  • Swarm (bees, wasps, midges, bats, etc.)
  • Tall tree to climb, and a reason to climb it

Creatures the bestiary catalogues in and around The Great Wood. Click through for full stat blocks and lore.
